project.name: Plumbing Directory — Programmatic SEO Multi-Site Generator
project.summary: A system that generates geo-targeted plumbing service pages for every US city × 150 services, deployed as Cloudflare Workers. Built to scale: one template repo powers unlimited independently-branded sites, each with unique AI-generated content, deployed fully automatically via GitHub Actions.
project.created_for: A single operator (aroragroupindiamart-gif on GitHub) running multiple plumbing directory sites under different brand names, all on the Cloudflare free plan.

first_live_site.status: Live and indexed by Google Search Console

architecture.overview: A pnpm monorepo with two main parts: (1) a Next.js 15 site template (artifacts/plumbing-directory) that is deployed as a GitHub template repo, and (2) a scripts package (scripts/src/site-factory) containing automation tooling to spin up new sites from that template.

monorepo_layout.artifacts/plumbing-directory: The Next.js 15 site template — every new site is cloned from this.
monorepo_layout.scripts/src/site-factory: Automation scripts to create repos, wire Cloudflare, and generate unique content per site.
Master registry of every site ever launched — siteId, domain, repo URL, Cloudflare zone ID, content salt.

deployment_stack.adapter: @opennextjs/cloudflare (OpenNext) — wraps Next.js output for Cloudflare Workers runtime
deployment_stack.hosting: Cloudflare Workers (NOT Cloudflare Pages — each site is a Worker script)
deployment_stack.ci_cd: GitHub Actions — deploy.yml triggers on every push to main, builds and deploys via wrangler-action
deployment_stack.rendering: State + city pages: statically generated at build time. Service detail pages: rendered on demand by the Worker, cached at the edge.
deployment_stack.why_not_static_export: Full static export (~201k HTML files) takes 15-45 minutes to build. Cloudflare Workers + OpenNext lets service pages render on-demand so CI stays fast.

the_site_template.what_it_generates: One website per plumbing brand. Each site covers all US cities × up to 150 plumbing services = up to ~201,000 unique SEO pages.

dataset.locations: 2,249 US cities in lib/data/locations.json — each has: id, state_code, state_name, place_name, place_slug, population, tier (1/2/3)
dataset.services: 150 plumbing services in lib/data/services.json — each has: id, service_name, service_slug, base_keywords[]

tier_system.explanation: Cities are ranked by population into 3 tiers. Tier determines how many of the 150 services a city gets on its page and in generateStaticParams.

page_structure.homepage: / — 50-state grid showing city counts and total page counts. JSON-LD for Organization + WebSite schema.
page_structure.state_page: /{state} — e.g. /ca — grid of all cities in that state sorted by population.
page_structure.city_page: /{state}/{city} — e.g. /ca/los-angeles — grid of services available for that city's tier.
page_structure.service_page: /{state}/{city}/{service} — e.g. /ca/los-angeles/drain-cleaning — full SEO page with: title/description meta, canonical URL, JSON-LD LocalBusiness schema, star ratings, intro paragraph, body paragraphs, trust bullet points, FAQ section, links to nearby cities and sibling services.

content_system.content_pack: lib/data/content-pack.json — sentence-level templates with {placeholder} tokens. Has pools for: intro paragraphs, body1, body2, trust bullet sets, FAQ answers (cost/arrival/before-arrival).
content_system.spintax_selection: lib/spintax.ts selectVariant() picks which template a page uses via a deterministic hash: Math.abs((locationId * 31 + serviceId + salt * 97) % variants.length). Same URL always gets the same content variant. Salt=0 is reserved for the original template site.
content_system.content_rendering: lib/content.ts renderTemplate() substitutes {placeholder} tokens with real values at render time.

seo_features: JSON-LD structured data (LocalBusiness, Organization, WebSite, Service schemas)
Star ratings (4.7–5.0, deterministically varied per city/service)
Cross-links to nearby cities (3 per page) and sibling services (5 per page)
Sitemap index at /sitemap.xml linking to per-state sitemaps at /sitemap/{state}
robots.txt served from app/robots.ts — allows all, disallows /_next/ and RSC params, includes Sitemap directive
Google Search Console verification via NEXT_PUBLIC_GSC_TOKEN meta tag

key_components.MobileStickyBar.tsx: Bottom call-to-action bar on mobile (client component)

config_surface.source: lib/config.ts reads NEXT_PUBLIC_* environment variables at build time

variables.NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_NAME: Brand name shown throughout the site
variables.NEXT_PUBLIC_PHONE_TEL: Digits-only phone for tel: links, e.g. 8005550000
variables.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: Full domain with protocol, e.g. https://victormoodyplumbinginc.com
variables.NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_SALT: Per-site spintax offset integer — 0 for the template, non-zero for every generated site

important_bugs_fixed.bug: Sitemap index linked to /sitemap/{state}.xml (with .xml extension) but the actual route serves at /sitemap/{state} (without .xml). This caused Google Search Console to report 'Couldn't fetch' for all state sitemaps.
important_bugs_fixed.fix: Removed .xml suffix from <loc> URLs in app/sitemap.xml/route.ts

important_bugs_fixed.bug: A stale public/robots.txt file (containing only 'User-agent: *\nAllow: /') was shadowing the correct app/robots.ts (which has proper Disallow rules and the Sitemap: directive). Next.js serves public/ files before app/ route handlers.
important_bugs_fixed.fix: Deleted public/robots.txt so the correct app/robots.ts is served

site_factory.purpose: Automate spinning up a new independently-branded plumbing directory site in minutes instead of a full manual setup session. Each new site gets its own GitHub repo, Cloudflare Worker, custom domain binding, and AI-generated unique content.

commands.site:create-repo: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:create-repo -- path/to/config.json — creates GitHub repo from template, sets all secrets, generates unique content pack
commands.site:setup-domain: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:setup-domain -- path/to/config.json — registers Cloudflare zone, polls until active, binds custom domain to Worker
commands.site:find: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:find -- domain.com — looks up a site in the registry by domain
commands.site:generate-content-pack: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:generate-content-pack -- path/to/config.json — standalone content pack generation (retry command)

what_it_does: Marks the template repo as is_template on GitHub (if not already)
Calls POST /repos/{templateRepo}/generate to create a new independent GitHub repo
Enables GitHub Actions on the new repo
Calls siteContentSalt(siteId) to derive a deterministic non-zero salt
Writes the new site to sites-registry.json
Sets all required GitHub Actions secrets (brand, phone, domain, Cloudflare creds, content salt) using libsodium sealed-box encryption via the GitHub Secrets API
Waits for the new repo's file tree to become queryable (GitHub's template generation is async)
Calls generateContentPack() to get AI-written content templates
Commits the new content-pack.json to the new repo's lib/data/content-pack.json
The initial commit from template generation triggers the deploy.yml GitHub Actions workflow automatically — no manual trigger needed
Calls GET /zones?name={domain} to check if the zone already exists
If not: calls POST /zones to create the Cloudflare zone for the domain
Prints the two Cloudflare nameservers and waits (polling every 30s, up to 20 attempts) for the zone to go active
NOTE: Nameserver update at the domain registrar is MANUAL — the script cannot do this
Calls PUT /accounts/{accountId}/workers/domains to bind the domain as a Custom Domain to the Worker (Cloudflare then manages the DNS A record automatically)
Updates sites-registry.json with the Cloudflare zone ID
dependencies: CLOUDFLARE_DNS_API_TOKEN (separate from deploy token — needs Zone+DNS+Edit, Zone+Zone+Edit scoped to All zones, Account+Account Settings+Edit, Account+Workers Scripts+Edit)

what_it_does: Builds a prompt instructing Claude to write sentence-level marketing copy templates for the site's niche and brand
Calls Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6 via the Replit AI Integrations proxy (no personal API key needed)
Validates the response with Zod schema (requires at least 6 intro, 6 body1, 6 body2, 4 trust bullet sets of 5 each, 3 FAQ answers per question)
Validates that no disallowed placeholder tokens were used
Returns the content pack object for create-site-repo.ts to commit

output_shape.trustBulletSets: array of bullet-point arrays (at least 4 sets, 5 bullets each)
output_shape.faqCostAnswers: array of FAQ answer templates (at least 3)
output_shape.faqArrivalAnswers: array of FAQ answer templates (at least 3)
output_shape.faqBeforeArrivalAnswers: array of FAQ answer templates (at least 3)

scripts.site-registry.ts: upsertSiteEntry() / registryEntryFromConfig() — read/write helpers for sites-registry.json
scripts.find-site.ts: CLI: reads sites-registry.json and prints matching site(s) by domain or lists all

types.ts.SiteConfig: Interface defining all per-site config fields (siteId, githubOwner, templateRepo, brandName, phoneNumber, phoneTel, domain, niche fields, optional GA/AdSense/GSC IDs)
types.ts.siteContentSalt: function(siteId: string): number — deterministic hash of siteId → non-zero integer salt. Salt=0 is reserved for the live template. Formula: hash = chars.reduce((h, c) => (h*31 + c) % 100000, 0); return (hash % 9973) + 1
types.ts.toRepoSecrets: function(config, cfAccountId, cfApiToken): Record<string,string> — maps SiteConfig to the full set of GitHub Actions secrets

content_differentiation_strategy.problem: All sites share the same city/service dataset and page templates. Without differentiation, every site would render byte-identical sentences for the same city/service pair — a duplicate content SEO penalty risk.
content_differentiation_strategy.solution_layer_1_salt: Each site gets a unique NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_SALT (derived from siteId). The selectVariant() hash includes salt, so even sites sharing the same content pool pick different variants per page.
content_differentiation_strategy.solution_layer_2_content_pack: Each new site gets a fully AI-generated content-pack.json with genuinely different wording (not synonym-swapped) tailored to that site's brand/niche. Committed to the site's repo at creation time.
content_differentiation_strategy.hard_constraint: The live template site's content-pack.json must NEVER be changed (no entries added, removed, or reordered) and its CONTENT_SALT must stay 0. Changing either one changes which variant every existing page renders, corrupting the live site's output. generateContentPack must NEVER be run against the template repo.
content_differentiation_strategy.residual_risk: Page structure, JSON-LD shape, dataset facts (city name, service name), and FAQ question wording are still shared across all sites. Only marketing copy sentences vary.

steps: pnpm run build:cf (builds via OpenNext for Cloudflare Workers, NODE_OPTIONS max-old-space-size=6144)
cloudflare/wrangler-action@v4 — deploys the built Worker to Cloudflare
github_actions_pipeline.why_public_repos: GitHub Actions minutes are unlimited for public repos. Private repos share a 2,000 min/month cap across the account — unworkable at 30+ sites doing regular deploys.
github_actions_pipeline.secrets_encryption: Secrets are set programmatically via GitHub Secrets API using libsodium sealed-box encryption (the same pattern GitHub's own web UI uses internally).

cloudflare_setup.architecture_per_site: 1 Cloudflare zone (domain) + 1 Worker script + 1 Custom Domain binding
cloudflare_setup.custom_domain_vs_route: Custom Domain binding (not a Worker route) — Cloudflare manages the DNS A record automatically once the domain is bound. No manual DNS record creation needed after nameserver delegation.

free_plan_limits.workers_per_account: 100 total (hard cap) — this is the primary scaling ceiling since each site = 1 Worker
free_plan_limits.requests_per_day: 100,000 shared across ALL Workers in the account — the second scaling ceiling once sites gain traffic

cloudflare_setup.safe_site_count_per_account: Recommended: 70-80 sites per free Cloudflare account. Do not push to 100 (no buffer for retries/mistakes). Switch to a new account when either the site count approaches 80 OR combined daily requests approach 50,000-60,000/day (not 100,000 — traffic spikes need headroom).
cloudflare_setup.account_switch_strategy: When near limits, start a new free Cloudflare account for the next batch of sites. No billing upgrade needed — just use different account credentials.

api_token_permissions.deploy_token_CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: Deploy-only — used by GitHub Actions via wrangler-action. Does not have zone/DNS permissions.

required_permissions: Zone → Zone → Edit scoped to All zones (required to create new zones — chicken-and-egg: can't scope to a zone that doesn't exist yet)
Account → Account Settings → Edit (also required for zone creation)
Account → Workers Scripts → Edit (required for PUT /accounts/{id}/workers/domains — missing this causes misleading 403 code 10000 that looks like a bad token but is actually a missing-scope error)

google_search_console.sitemap_format: Sitemap index linking to per-state sitemaps at /sitemap/{state} (no .xml extension in the URL)
google_search_console.verification_method: HTML meta tag — NEXT_PUBLIC_GSC_TOKEN rendered in <head> as <meta name='google-site-verification' content='...'/>
google_search_console.note_on_sitemap_not_indexed: URL Inspection in GSC showing 'not indexed' for /sitemap/ak or similar is NORMAL — sitemap XML files are not indexed as search results. The correct check is 'Test Live URL' which confirms Google can fetch the file (HTTP 200).

google_analytics.implementation: gtag.js loaded in root layout via NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID — renders as global site tag in <head>

how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_1: Copy scripts/src/site-factory/example-site-config.json and fill in: siteId (machine-friendly, becomes repo + Worker name), brandName, phoneNumber, phoneTel, domain. Keep niche fields as-is for plumbing.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_2: Run: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:create-repo -- path/to/your-config.json. This creates the GitHub repo, sets all secrets, and generates a unique content pack. GitHub Actions starts deploying automatically.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_3: Buy the domain from any registrar.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_4: Run: pnpm --filter @workspace/scripts run site:setup-domain -- path/to/your-config.json. This creates the Cloudflare zone and prints the two nameservers you need to set at your registrar.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_5: At your domain registrar, update the nameservers to the two Cloudflare nameservers printed in step 4. This is the ONLY manual step.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_6: The setup-domain script polls automatically until the zone goes active (can take minutes to hours depending on registrar). Once active, it binds the domain to the Worker.
how_to_launch_a_new_site.step_7: Site is live at https://domain. Optional: add GA ID and GSC token as additional GitHub repo secrets, then push a commit to redeploy.

in_replit.GITHUB_PAT: Personal access token with repo + workflow scopes — used by all factory scripts to create repos, set secrets, push files
in_replit.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: Cloudflare account ID — shared across all sites in the same CF account
in_replit.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: Deploy-only Cloudflare token — reused as a GitHub Actions secret on every generated site repo
in_replit.CLOUDFLARE_DNS_API_TOKEN: Zone/DNS/Workers-domain management token — used by setup-cloudflare-domain.ts only
in_replit.SESSION_SECRET: Used by the API server artifact (separate from plumbing-directory)

environment_variables_and_secrets.per_site_in_github_actions: All NEXT_PUBLIC_* variables listed above, plus CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID — set automatically by create-site-repo.ts

what_is_shared_across_all_sites: All app code (app/, components/, lib/ except content-pack.json)
what_varies_per_site: siteId (GitHub repo name, Cloudflare Worker name)
GA ID, AdSense ID, GSC verification token (optional, per-site)
known_limits_and_gotchas: Do NOT run pnpm run build locally without patience — generates ~201k HTML files, takes 15-45 minutes.
Do NOT run generate-content-pack.ts against the live template repo or hand-edit its content-pack.json arrays — changing array length or order changes which variant every existing page renders.
middleware.ts is NOT supported with output:export — never add it to a static export site.
CONTENT_SALT=0 is reserved for the original template site. All generated sites must have a non-zero salt.
Cloudflare zone creation is async — the zone appears immediately but nameserver propagation takes time. The setup-domain script polls and waits.
GitHub template repo generation is also async — create-site-repo.ts waits up to 20 seconds (polling) before trying to read the new repo's file tree.
All 100 Workers in a Cloudflare free account share the 100,000 requests/day quota — a traffic spike on one site can throttle all others.

The Cloudflare token scope needed for Workers Custom Domain binding is 'Account > Workers Scripts > Edit', NOT 'Account > Workers Custom Domains > Edit' (the latter does not exist as a separate permission in the dashboard). Using the wrong name causes a misleading 403.
Per-state sitemaps are served at /sitemap/{state} WITHOUT .xml extension. The sitemap index must link to URLs without .xml or Google gets 404s.
